title: Find percents visually
description: Use double number lines and tape diagrams to find missing parts, wholes, and percentages.

assessment_items.question_content:
The double number line shows that Henrik has a mass of $60\,\text{kg}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of Henrik's mass.**

Mass ($\text{kg}$) | Percentage
-:|-:
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] | $100\%$ of $60\,\text{kg}$
[[☃ numeric-input 2]] | $20\%$ of $60\,\text{kg}$
[[☃ numeric-input 3]] | $40\%$ of $60\,\text{kg}$
hints:
The number lines show that the ratio of $\maroonD{\text{kilograms}}$ to $\blueE{\text{percentage}}$ is $\maroonD{60}:\blueE{100\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\maroonD{60}:\blueE{100\%}$.

$\blueE{100}\div\blueE{20} = \purpleE5$

So. we can find the mass for $\blueE{20\%}$ by dividing both parts of the ratio by $\purpleE{5}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{20\%}$ of $\maroonD{60}$ is $\maroonD{12}$.

Now we can find the mass for $\blueE{40\%}$ by multiplying both parts of the new ratio by $\purpleE{2}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{40\%}$ of $\maroonD{60}$ is $\maroonD{24}$.

###Completed table

Mass ($\text{kg}$) | Percentage
-:|-:
$60$ | $100\%$ of $60\,\text{kg}$
$12$ | $20\%$ of $60\,\text{kg}$
$24$ | $40\%$ of $60\,\text{kg}$

options.alt: A double number line with 2 tick marks. The line labeled Mass, kilograms, reads from left to right: 0, 60. The line labeled Percentage, reads from left to right: 0 percent, 100 percent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The double number line shows that a traffic signal allows traffic through the intersection for $36$ seconds.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of the time the signal allows.**

Time ($\text{s}$) | Percentage
-:|-:
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] | $100\%$ of $36\,\text{s}$
[[☃ numeric-input 2]] | $25\%$ of $36\,\text{s}$
[[☃ numeric-input 3]] | $125\%$ of $36\,\text{s}$
hints:
The number lines show that the ratio of $\maroonD{\text{seconds}}$ to $\blueE{\text{percentage}}$ is $\maroonD{36}:\blueE{100\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\maroonD{36}:\blueE{100\%}$.

$\blueE{100}\div\blueE{25} = \purpleE{4}$

So. we can find the time for $\blueE{25\%}$ by dividing both parts of the ratio by $\purpleE{4}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{25\%}$ of $\maroonD{36}$ is $\maroonD{9}$.

Now we can find the time for $\blueE{125\%}$ by multiplying both parts of the new ratio by $\purpleE{5}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{125\%}$ of $\maroonD{36}$ is $\maroonD{45}$.

This makes sense because $\blueE{125\%}$ is more than $\blueE{100\%}$. So it should represent more than $\maroonD{36\,\text{s}}$.

###Completed table

Time ($\text{s}$) | Percentage
-:|-:
$36$ | $100\%$ of $36\,\text{s}$
$9$ | $25\%$ of $36\,\text{s}$
$45$ | $125\%$ of $36\,\text{s}$

options.alt: A double number line with 2 tick marks. The line labeled Time, seconds, reads from left to right: 0, 36. The line labeled Percentage, reads from left to right: 0 percent, 100 percent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The double number line shows that $42$ people watched Deon's new video today.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of the number of people who watched the video.**

People | Percentage
-:|-:
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] | $100\%$ of $42$ people
[[☃ numeric-input 2]] | $50\%$ of $42$ people
[[☃ numeric-input 3]] | $150\%$ of $42$ people
hints:
The number lines show that the ratio of $\maroonD{\text{people}}$ to $\blueE{\text{percentage}}$ is $\maroonD{42}:\blueE{100\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\maroonD{42}:\blueE{100\%}$.

$\blueE{100}\div\blueE{50} = \purpleE{2}$

So. we can find the people for $\blueE{50\%}$ by dividing both parts of the ratio by $\purpleE{2}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{50\%}$ of $\maroonD{42}$ is $\maroonD{21}$.

Now we can find the people for $\blueE{150\%}$ by multiplying both parts of the new ratio by $\purpleE{3}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{150\%}$ of $\maroonD{42}$ is $\maroonD{63}$.

This makes sense because $\blueE{150\%}$ is more than $\blueE{100\%}$. So it should represent more than $\maroonD{42}$ people.

###Completed table

People | Percentage
-:|-:
$42$ | $100\%$ of $42$ people
$21$ | $50\%$ of $42$ people
$63$ | $150\%$ of $42$ people

options.alt: A double number line with 2 tick marks. The line labeled People, reads from left to right: 0, 42. The line labeled Percentage, reads from left to right: 0 percent, 100 percent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The double number line shows that Kimimela fed her rabbits $350$ days this year. Her foster brother fed them the other days.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of the days that Kimimela fed her rabbits.**

Time (days) | Percentage
-:|-:
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] | $100\%$ of $350$ days
[[☃ numeric-input 2]] | $20\%$ of $350$ days
[[☃ numeric-input 3]] | $80\%$ of $350$ days
hints:
The number lines show that the ratio of $\maroonD{\text{days}}$ to $\blueE{\text{percentage}}$ is $\maroonD{350}:\blueE{100\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\maroonD{350}:\blueE{100\%}$.

$\blueE{100}\div\blueE{20} = \purpleE{5}$

So. we can find the time for $\blueE{20\%}$ by dividing both parts of the ratio by $\purpleE{5}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{20\%}$ of $\maroonD{350}$ is $\maroonD{70}$.

Now we can find the time for $\blueE{80\%}$ by multiplying both parts of the new ratio by $\purpleE{4}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{80\%}$ of $\maroonD{350}$ is $\maroonD{280}$.

###Completed table

Time (days) | Percentage
-:|-:
$350$ | $100\%$ of $350$ days
$70$ | $20\%$ of $350$ days
$280$ | $80\%$ of $350$ days

options.alt: A double number line with 2 tick marks. The line labeled Time, days, reads from left to right: 0, 350. The line labeled Percentage, reads from left to right: 0 percent, 100 percent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The tape diagram shows that Bolívar takes $40$ minutes to steam a batch of tamales.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of the steaming time.**

Time ($\text{min}$) | Percentage
-:|-:
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] | $100\%$ of $40\,\text{min}$
[[☃ numeric-input 2]] | $50\%$ of $40\,\text{min}$
[[☃ numeric-input 3]] | $250\%$ of $40\,\text{min}$
hints:
The tape diagram shows that the ratio of minutes to percentage is $\purpleD{40}:\purpleD{100\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\purpleD{40}:\purpleD{100\%}$.

###Finding $\purpleD{100\%}$ of $40$ minutes

$\purpleD{100\%}$ is the whole time, which is $\purpleD{40}$ minutes.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueE{50\%}$ is $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{2}}$ of the time.  [[☃ explanation 1]]

$\maroonE{250\%}$ is $\maroonE{\dfrac{5}{2}}$ of the time.  [[☃ explanation 2]]

Now, we can use the same tape diagram to find $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{2}}$ of $\purpleD{40}$ minutes and $\maroonE{\dfrac{5}{2}}$ of $\purpleD{40}$ minutes.

###Finding $\blueE{50\%}$ of $40$ minutes

Each part in the tape diagram is $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{2}}$ of $\purpleD{100\%}$. So it's also $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{2}}$ of $\purpleD{40}$ minutes.

$\purpleD{40}\times \dfrac{1}{2} = {20}$

Each part represents ${20}$ minutes.

[[☃ image 2]]

So, the ratio $\purpleD{40}:\purpleD{100\%}$ is equivalent to $\blueE{20}:\blueE{50\%}$.

In other words, $\blueE{50\%}$ of $\purpleE{40}$ is $\blueE{20}$.

###Finding $\maroonE{250\%}$ of $40$ minutes

$\maroonE{250\%}$ is $\maroonE{\dfrac{5}{2}}$ of the time.

So it takes $ {5}$ parts to represent $\maroonE{250\%}$ of $\purpleD{40}$ minutes.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each part represents ${20}$ minutes.

$\blueE{20}\times {5} = \maroonE{100}$

So, the ratio $\purpleD{40}:\purpleD{100\%}$ is equivalent to $\maroonE{100}:\maroonE{250\%}$.

In other words, $\maroonE{250\%}$ of $\purpleE{40}$ is $\maroonE{100}$.

This makes sense because $\maroonE{250\%}$ is more than $\purpleD{100\%}$. So it should represent more than $\purpleD{40}$ minutes.

###Completed table

Time ($\text{min}$) | Percentage
-:|-:
$\purpleD{40}$ | $\purpleE{100\%}$ of $40\,\text{min}$
$\blueE{20}$ | $\blueE{50\%}$ of $40\,\text{min}$
$\maroonE{100}$ | $\maroonE{250\%}$ of $40\,\text{min}$

options.alt: A tape diagram with one tape split into 5 equal parts. The first 2 parts are shaded. A curved brace above the first 2 parts is labeled 40. A curved brace below the first 2 parts is labeled 100 percent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The tape diagram shows that Shanice spent $120$ minutes researching for debate club last week.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of Shanice's research time.**

Time ($\text{min}$) | Percentage
-:|-:
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] | $100\%$ of $120\,\text{min}$
[[☃ numeric-input 2]] | $10\%$ of $120\,\text{min}$
[[☃ numeric-input 3]] | $110\%$ of $120\,\text{min}$
hints:
The tape diagram shows that the ratio of minutes to percentage is $\purpleD{120}:\purpleD{100\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\purpleD{120}:\purpleD{100\%}$.

###Finding $\purpleD{100\%}$ of $\purpleD{120}$ minutes

$\purpleD{100\%}$ is the whole time, which is $\purpleD{120}$ minutes.

[[☃ image 1]]

Now, we can use the same tape diagram to find $\blueE{10\%}$ of $\purpleD{120}$ minutes and $\maroonE{110\%}$ of $\purpleD{120}$ minutes.

###Finding $\blueE{10\%}$ of $\purpleD{120}$ minutes

$\blueE{10\%}$ is $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{10}}$ of the time. [[☃ explanation 1]]

$\purpleD{120}\times \blueE{\dfrac{1}{10}} = \blueE{12}$

Each part represents $\blueE{12}$ minutes.

[[☃ image 2]]

So, the ratio $\purpleD{120}:\purpleD{100\%}$ is equivalent to $\blueE{12}:\blueE{10\%}$.

In other words, $\blueE{10\%}$ of $\purpleD{120}$ is $\blueE{12}$.

###Finding $\maroonE{110\%}$ of $\purpleD{120}$ minutes

$\maroonE{110\%}$ is $\maroonE{\dfrac{11}{10}}$ of the time. [[☃ explanation 1]]

So it takes ${11}$ parts to represent $\maroonE{110\%}$ of $\purpleD{120}$ minutes.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each part represents $\blueE{12}$ minutes.

$\blueE{12}\times {11} = \maroonE{132}$

So, the ratio $\purpleD{120}:\purpleD{100\%}$ is equivalent to $\maroonE{132}:\maroonE{110\%}$.

In other words, $\maroonE{110\%}$ of $\purpleE{120}$ is $\maroonE{132}$.

This makes sense because $\maroonE{110\%}$ is more than $\purpleD{100\%}$. So it should represent more than $\purpleD{120}$ minutes.

###Completed table

Time ($\text{min}$) | Percentage
-:|-:
$\purpleD{120}$ | $\purpleD{100\%}$ of $\purpleD{120\,\text{min}}$
$\blueE{12}$ | $\blueE{10\%}$ of $\purpleD{120\,\text{min}}$
$\maroonE{132}$ | $\maroonE{110\%}$ of $\purpleD{120\,\text{min}}$

options.alt: A tape diagram with one tape split into 11 equal parts. The first 10 parts are shaded. A curved brace above the first 10 parts is labeled 120. A curved brace below the first 10 parts is labeled 100 percent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The tape diagram shows that there are $240$ students graduating from a high school this year.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of the graduating students.**

Students | Percentage
-:|-:
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] | $100\%$ of $240$ students
[[☃ numeric-input 2]] | $25\%$ of $240$ students
[[☃ numeric-input 3]] | $75\%$ of $240$ students
hints:
The tape diagram shows that the ratio of students to percentage is $\purpleD{240}:\purpleD{100\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\purpleD{240}:\purpleD{100\%}$.

###Finding $\purpleD{100\%}$ of $\purpleD{240}$ students

$\purpleD{100\%}$ is the whole group, which is $\purpleD{240}$ students.

[[☃ image 1]]

Now, we can use the same tape diagram to find $\blueE{25\%}$ of $\purpleD{240}$ students and $\maroonE{75\%}$ of $\purpleD{240}$ students.

###Finding $\blueE{25\%}$ of $\purpleD{240}$ students

$\blueE{25\%}$ is $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{4}}$ of the students. [[☃ explanation 1]]

$\purpleD{240}\times \blueE{\dfrac{1}{4}} = \blueE{60}$

Each part represents $\blueE{60}$ students.

[[☃ image 2]]

So, the ratio $\purpleD{240}:\purpleD{100\%}$ is equivalent to $\blueE{60}:\blueE{25\%}$.

In other words, $\blueE{25\%}$ of $\purpleD{240}$ is $\blueE{60}$.

###Finding $\maroonE{75\%}$ of $\purpleD{240}$ students

$\maroonE{75\%}$ is $\maroonE{\dfrac{3}{4}}$ of the students. [[☃ explanation 1]]

So it takes ${3}$ parts to represent $\maroonE{75\%}$ of $\purpleD{240}$ students.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each part represents $\blueE{60}$ students.

$\blueE{60}\times {3} = \maroonE{180}$

So, the ratio $\purpleD{240}:\purpleD{100\%}$ is equivalent to $\maroonE{180}:\maroonE{75\%}$.

In other words, $\maroonE{75\%}$ of $\purpleE{240}$ is $\maroonE{180}$.

###Completed table

Students | Percentage
-:|-:
$\purpleD{240}$ | $\purpleD{100\%}$ of $\purpleD{240}$ students
$\blueE{60}$ | $\blueE{25\%}$ of $\purpleD{240}$ students
$\maroonE{180}$ | $\maroonE{75\%}$ of $\purpleD{240}$ students

options.alt: A tape diagram with one tape split into 4 equal parts. All 4 parts are shaded. A curved brace above the whole tape is labeled 240. A curved brace below the whole tape is labeled 100 percent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The double number line shows that Hiawatha, who is $24$ years old, is $75\%$ as old as Itzel is.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of Itzel's age.**

Age (years) | Percentage
-:|-:
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] | $75\%$ of Itzel's age
[[☃ numeric-input 2]] | $25\%$ of Itzel's age
[[☃ numeric-input 3]] | $100\%$ of Itzel's age
hints:
The number lines show that the ratio of $\maroonD{\text{years}}$ to $\blueE{\text{percentage}}$ is $\maroonD{24}:\blueE{75\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\maroonD{24}:\blueE{75\%}$.

We can find the age for $\blueE{25\%}$ by dividing both parts of the ratio by $\purpleE{3}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{25\%}$ of Itzel's age is $\maroonD{8}$ years.

Now we can find the age for $\blueE{100\%}$ by multiplying both parts of the new ratio by $\purpleE{4}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{100\%}$ of Itzel's is $\maroonD{32}$ years. That means Itzel is $\maroonD{32}$ years old.

###Completed table

Age (years) | Percentage
-:|-:
$24$ | $75\%$ of Itzel's age
$8$ | $25\%$ of Itzel's age
$32$ | $100\%$ of Itzel's age

options.alt: A double number line with 2 tick marks. The line labeled Age, years, reads from left to right: 0, 24. The line labeled Percentage, reads from left to right: 0 percent, 75 percent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The double number line shows that Bira has ridden $28\,\text{m}$ on a [[☃ definition 1]], which is $80\%$ of a full rotation on the carousel.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of a full rotation on the carousel.**

Distance ($\text{m}$) | Percentage
-:|-:
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] | $80\%$ of a rotation
[[☃ numeric-input 2]] | $20\%$ of a rotation
[[☃ numeric-input 3]] | $100\%$ of a rotation
hints:
The number lines show that the ratio of $\maroonD{\text{meters}}$ to $\blueE{\text{percentage}}$ is $\maroonD{28}:\blueE{80\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\maroonD{28}:\blueE{80\%}$.

We can find the distance for $\blueE{20\%}$ by dividing both parts of the ratio by $\purpleE{4}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{20\%}$ of a rotation is $\maroonD{7\,\text{m}}$.

Now we can find the distance for $\blueE{100\%}$ by multiplying both parts of the new ratio by $\purpleE{5}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{100\%}$ of a rotation is $\maroonD{35\,\text{m}}$. That means Bira will ride $\maroonD{35\,\text{m}}$ in a full rotation of the carousel.

###Completed table

Distance ($\text{m}$) | Percentage
-:|-:
$28$ | $80\%$ of a rotation
$7$ | $20\%$ of a rotation
$35$ | $100\%$ of a rotation

options.definition: A spinning machine with seats or wooden animals that people ride for fun.

options.alt: A double number line with 2 tick marks. The line labeled Distance, meters, reads from left to right: 0, 28. The line labeled Percentage, reads from left to right: 0 percent, 80 percent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The double number line shows that $270$ people attended this year's auction, which is $125\%$ as many people as attended last year.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of last year's attendance.**

People | Percentage
-:|-:
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] | $125\%$ of last year's attendance
[[☃ numeric-input 2]] | $25\%$ of last year's attendance
[[☃ numeric-input 3]] | $100\%$ of last year's attendance
hints:
The number lines show that the ratio of $\maroonD{\text{people}}$ to $\blueE{\text{percentage}}$ is $\maroonD{270}:\blueE{125\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\maroonD{270}:\blueE{125\%}$.

We can find the number of people for $\blueE{25\%}$ by dividing both parts of the ratio by $\purpleE{5}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{25\%}$ of last year's attendance is $\maroonD{54}$ people.

Now we can find the number of people for $\blueE{100\%}$ by multiplying both parts of the new ratio by $\purpleE{4}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{100\%}$ of last year's attendance is $\maroonD{216}$ people. That means $\maroonD{216}$ people attended the auction last year.

###Completed table

People | Percentage
-:|-:
$270$ | $125\%$ of last year's attendance
$54$ | $25\%$ of last year's attendance
$216$ | $100\%$ of last year's attendance

options.alt: A double number line with 2 tick marks. The line labeled People, reads from left to right: 0, 270. The line labeled Percentage, reads from left to right: 0 percent, 125 percent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The tape diagram shows that Ehecatl's hair, which is now $12\,\text{cm}$ long, is $60\%$ as long as it was before his haircut.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of Ehecatl's hair length before the haircut.**

Length ($\text{cm}$) | Percentage
-:|-:
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] | $60\%$ of Ehecatl's old hair length
[[☃ numeric-input 2]] | $20\%$ of Ehecatl's old hair length
[[☃ numeric-input 3]] | $100\%$ of Ehecatl's old hair length
hints:
The tape diagram shows that the ratio of length to percentage is $\purpleD{12}:\purpleD{60\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\purpleD{12}:\purpleD{60\%}$.

###Finding $\purpleD{60\%}$ of Ehecatl's old hair length

$\purpleD{60\%}$ of the old length is $\purpleD{12\,\text{cm}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Now, we can use the same tape diagram to find $\blueE{20\%}$ and $\maroonE{100\%}$ of the old length.

###Finding $\blueE{20\%}$ of Ehecatl's old hair length

Each part in the tape diagram is ${\dfrac{1}{3}}$ of $\purpleD{60\%}$ and ${\dfrac{1}{3}}$ of $\purpleD{12\,\text{cm}}$.

$\begin{align}
\purpleD{60\%}\times \dfrac{1}{3} &= \blueE{20\%}\\\\
\purpleD{12}\times \dfrac{1}{3} &= \blueE{4}
\end{align}$

Each part represents $\blueE{4\,\text{cm}}$.

[[☃ image 2]]

So, the ratio $\purpleD{12}:\purpleD{60\%}$ is equivalent to $\blueE{4}:\blueE{20\%}$.

In other words, $\blueE{20\%}$ of Ehecatl's old hair length is $\blueE{4\,\text{cm}}$.

###Finding $\maroonE{100\%}$ of Ehecatl's old hair length

$\blueE{20\%} \times 5 = \maroonE{100\%}$

So it takes ${5}$ parts to represent $\maroonE{100\%}$ of Ehecatl's old hair length.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each part represents $\blueE{4\,\text{cm}}$.

$\blueE{4}\times {5} = \maroonE{20}$

So, the ratio $\purpleD{12}:\purpleD{60\%}$ is equivalent to $\maroonE{20}:\maroonE{100\%}$.

In other words, $\maroonE{100\%}$ of Ehecatl's old hair length is $\maroonE{20\,\text{cm}}$. That means Ehecatl's hair was $\maroonE{20\,\text{cm}}$ long before the haircut.

###Completed table

Length ($\text{cm}$) | Percentage
-:|-:
$\purpleD{12}$ | $\purpleD{60\%}$ of Ehecatl's old hair length
$\blueE{4}$ | $\blueE{20\%}$ of Ehecatl's old hair length
$\maroonE{20}$ | $\maroonE{100\%}$ of Ehecatl's old hair length

options.alt: A tape diagram with one tape split into 5 equal parts. The first 3 parts are shaded. A curved brace above the first 3 parts is labeled 12. A curved brace below the first 3 parts is labeled 60 percent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The tape diagram shows that Odalis is $132\,\text{cm}$ tall, which is $150\%$ of their sister Dalia's height.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of Dalia's height.**

Height ($\text{cm}$) | Percentage
-:|-:
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] | $150\%$ of Dalia's height
[[☃ numeric-input 2]] | $25\%$ of Dalia's height
[[☃ numeric-input 3]] | $100\%$ of Dalia's height
hints:
The tape diagram shows that the ratio of height to percentage is $\purpleD{132}:\purpleD{150\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\purpleD{132}:\purpleD{150\%}$.

###Finding $\purpleD{150\%}$ of Dalia's height

$\purpleD{150\%}$ of Dalia's height is $\purpleD{132\,\text{cm}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Now, we can use the same tape diagram to find $\blueE{25\%}$ and $\maroonE{100\%}$ of Dalia's height.

###Finding $\blueE{25\%}$ of Dalia's height

Each part in the tape diagram is ${\dfrac{1}{6}}$ of $\purpleD{150\%}$ and ${\dfrac{1}{6}}$ of $\purpleD{132\,\text{cm}}$.

$\begin{align}
\purpleD{150\%}\times \dfrac{1}{6} &= \blueE{25\%}\\\\
\purpleD{132}\times \dfrac{1}{6} &= \blueE{22}
\end{align}$

Each part represents $\blueE{22\,\text{cm}}$.

[[☃ image 2]]

So, the ratio $\purpleD{132}:\purpleD{150\%}$ is equivalent to $\blueE{22}:\blueE{25\%}$.

In other words, $\blueE{25\%}$ of Dalia's height is $\blueE{22\,\text{cm}}$.

###Finding $\maroonE{100\%}$ of Dalia's height

$\blueE{25\%} \times 4 = \maroonE{100\%}$

So it takes ${4}$ parts to represent $\maroonE{100\%}$ of Dalia's height.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each part represents $\blueE{22\,\text{cm}}$.

$\blueE{22}\times {4} = \maroonE{88}$

So, the ratio $\purpleD{132}:\purpleD{150\%}$ is equivalent to $\maroonE{88}:\maroonE{100\%}$.

In other words, $\maroonE{100\%}$ of Dalia's height is $\maroonE{88\,\text{cm}}$. That means Dalia is $\maroonE{88\,\text{cm}}$ tall.

###Completed table

Height ($\text{cm}$) | Percentage
-:|-:
$\purpleD{132}$ | $\purpleD{150\%}$ of Dalia's height
$\blueE{22}$ | $\blueE{25\%}$ of Dalia's height
$\maroonE{88}$ | $\maroonE{100\%}$ of Dalia's height

options.alt: A tape diagram with one tape split into 6 equal parts. All 6 parts are shaded. A curved brace above the whole tape is labeled 132. A curved brace below the whole tape is labeled 150 percent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The tape diagram shows that Elizabeth spent $91$ minutes, which was $70\%$ of her study time for her language exam, on conversation practice.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of Elizabeth's study time.**

Time (minutes) | Percentage
-:|-:
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] | $70\%$ of Elizabeth's study time
[[☃ numeric-input 2]] | $10\%$ of Elizabeth's study time
[[☃ numeric-input 3]] | $100\%$ of Elizabeth's study time
hints:
The tape diagram shows that the ratio of minutes to percentage is $\purpleD{91}:\purpleD{70\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\purpleD{91}:\purpleD{70\%}$.

###Finding $\purpleD{70\%}$ of Elizabeth's study time

$\purpleD{70\%}$ of the study time is $\purpleD{91}$ minutes.

[[☃ image 1]]

Now, we can use the same tape diagram to find $\blueE{10\%}$ and $\maroonE{100\%}$ of the study time.

###Finding $\blueE{10\%}$ of Elizabeth's study time

Each part in the tape diagram is ${\dfrac{1}{7}}$ of $\purpleD{70\%}$ and ${\dfrac{1}{7}}$ of $\purpleD{91}$ minutes.

$\begin{align}
\purpleD{70\%}\times \dfrac{1}{7} &= \blueE{10\%}\\\\
\purpleD{91}\times \dfrac{1}{7} &= \blueE{13}
\end{align}$

Each part represents $\blueE{13}$ minutes.

[[☃ image 2]]

So, the ratio $\purpleD{91}:\purpleD{70\%}$ is equivalent to $\blueE{13}:\blueE{10\%}$.

In other words, $\blueE{10\%}$ of Elizabeth's study time is $\blueE{13}$ minutes.

###Finding $\maroonE{100\%}$ of Elizabeth's study time

$\blueE{10\%} \times 10 = \maroonE{100\%}$

So it takes ${10}$ parts to represent $\maroonE{100\%}$ of Elizabeth's study time.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each part represents $\blueE{13}$ minutes.

$\blueE{13}\times {10} = \maroonE{130}$

So, the ratio $\purpleD{91}:\purpleD{70\%}$ is equivalent to $\maroonE{130}:\maroonE{100\%}$.

In other words, $\maroonE{100\%}$ of Elizabeth's study time is $\maroonE{130}$ minutes. That means Elizabeth studied for $\maroonE{130}$ minutes.

###Completed table

Time (minutes) | Percentage
-:|-:
$\purpleD{91}$ | $\purpleD{70\%}$ of Elizabeth's study time
$\blueE{13}$ | $\blueE{10\%}$ of Elizabeth's study time
$\maroonE{130}$ | $\maroonD{100\%}$ of Elizabeth's study time

options.alt: A tape diagram with one tape split into 10 equal parts. The first 7 parts are shaded. A curved brace above the first 7 parts is labeled 91. A curved brace below the first 7 parts is labeled 70 percent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The tape diagram shows that Rita answered $14$ questions correctly on a post-test. Rita learned a lot in this unit, so that was $350\%$ as many questions as she answered correctly in the pre-test.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of the questions Rita answered correctly on the pre-test.**

Number of questions correct | Percentage
-:|-:
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] | $350\%$ of the correct pre-test questions
[[☃ numeric-input 2]] | $50\%$ of the correct pre-test questions
[[☃ numeric-input 3]] | $100\%$ of the correct pre-test questions
hints:
The tape diagram shows that the ratio of questions to percentage is $\purpleD{14}:\purpleD{350\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\purpleD{14}:\purpleD{350\%}$.

###Finding $\purpleD{350\%}$ of the correct pre-test questions

$\purpleD{350\%}$ of Rita's correct pre-test question is $\purpleD{14}$ questions.

[[☃ image 1]]

Now, we can use the same tape diagram to find $\blueE{50\%}$ and $\maroonE{100\%}$ of the correct pre-test questions.

###Finding $\blueE{50\%}$ of the correct pre-test questions

Each part in the tape diagram is ${\dfrac{1}{7}}$ of $\purpleD{350\%}$ and ${\dfrac{1}{7}}$ of $\purpleD{14}$ questions.

$\begin{align}
\purpleD{350\%}\times \dfrac{1}{7} &= \blueE{50\%}\\\\
\purpleD{14}\times \dfrac{1}{7} &= \blueE{2}
\end{align}$

Each part represents $\blueE{2}$ questions.

[[☃ image 2]]

So, the ratio $\purpleD{14}:\purpleD{350\%}$ is equivalent to $\blueE{2}:\blueE{50\%}$.

In other words, $\blueE{50\%}$ of the questions Rita answered correctly on the pretest is $\blueE{2}$ questions.

###Finding $\maroonE{100\%}$ of the correct pre-test questions

$\blueE{50\%} \times 2 = \maroonE{100\%}$

So it takes ${2}$ parts to represent $\maroonE{100\%}$ of Rita's correct pre-test questions.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each part represents $\blueE{2}$ questions.

$\blueE{2}\times {2} = \maroonE{4}$

So, the ratio $\purpleD{14}:\purpleD{350\%}$ is equivalent to $\maroonE{4}:\maroonE{100\%}$.

In other words, $\maroonE{100\%}$ of the questions Rita answered correctly on the pre-test is $\maroonE{4}$ questions. That means Rita answered $\maroonE{4}$ questions correctly on the pre-test.

###Completed table

Number of questions correct | Percentage
-:|-:
$\purpleD{14}$ | $\purpleD{350\%}$ of the correct pre-test questions
$\blueE{2}$ | $\blueE{50\%}$ of the correct pre-test questions
$\maroonE{4}$ | $\maroonE{100\%}$ of the correct pre-test questions

options.alt: A tape diagram with one tape split into 7 equal parts. All 7 parts are shaded. A curved brace above the whole tape is labeled 14. A curved brace below the whole tape is labeled 350 percent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The double number line shows that Jéssica lives $48\,\text{km}$ from the capitol.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of the distance.**

Distance ($\text{km}$) | Percentage
:-:|:-:
$48$ | [[☃ numeric-input 1]]$\%$ of $48\,\text{km}$
$12$ | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]$\%$ of $48\,\text{km}$
$60$ | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]$\%$ of $48\,\text{km}$
hints:
The number lines show that the ratio of $\maroonD{\text{kilometers}}$ to $\blueE{\text{percentage}}$ is $\maroonD{48}:\blueE{100\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\maroonD{48}:\blueE{100\%}$.

$\maroonD{48}\div12= \purpleE4$

So, we can find the percentage for $12\,\text{km}$ by dividing both parts of the ratio by $\purpleE{4}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{25\%}$ of $48$ is $\maroonD{12}$.

$\maroonD{60}\div\maroonD{12}=\purpleE{5}$

Now we can find the percentage for $\maroonD{60\,\text{km}}$ by multiplying both parts of the new ratio by $\purpleE{5}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{125\%}$ of $48$ is $\maroonD{60}$.

This makes sense because $60\,\text{km}$ is farther than $48\,\text{km}$. So it should be more than $100\%$ of the distance.

###Completed table

Distance (km) | Percentage
-:|-:
$48$ | $100\%$ of $48\,\text{km}$
$12$ | $25\%$ of $48\,\text{km}$
$60$ | $125\%$ of $48\,\text{km}$

options.alt: A double number line with 2 tick marks. The line labeled Distance, kilometers, reads from left to right: 0, 48. The line labeled Percentage, reads from left to right: 0 percent, 100 percent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The double number line shows that Shanika has built $15$ banjos.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of the banjos.**

Banjos | Percentage
:-:|:-:
$15$ | [[☃ numeric-input 1]]$\%$ of $15$ banjos
$3$ | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]$\%$ of $15$ banjos
$6$ | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]$\%$ of $15$ banjos
hints:
The number lines show that the ratio of $\maroonD{\text{banjos}}$ to $\blueE{\text{percentage}}$ is $\maroonD{15}:\blueE{100\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\maroonD{15}:\blueE{100\%}$.

$\maroonD{15}\div \maroonD{3}= \purpleE{5}$

So, we can find the percentage for $\maroonD{3}$ by dividing both parts of the ratio by $\purpleE{5}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{20\%}$ of $15$ is $\maroonD{3}$.

$\maroonD{6}\div\maroonD{3}=\purpleE{2}$

Now we can find the percentage for $6$ banjos by multiplying both parts of the new ratio by $\purpleE{2}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{40\%}$ of $15$ is $\maroonD{6}$.

###Completed table

Banjos | Percentage
:-:|:-:
$15$ | $100\%$ of $15$ banjos
$3$ | $20\%$ of $15$ banjos
$6$ | $40\%$ of $15$ banjos

options.alt: A double number line with 2 tick marks. The line labeled Banjos, reads from left to right: 0, 15. The line labeled Percentage, reads from left to right: 0 percent, 100 percent.

assessment_items.question_content:

Games | Percentage

hints:

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\maroonD{250}:\blueE{100\%}$.

$\maroonD{250}\div \maroonD{50}= \purpleE{5}$

So, we can find the percentage for $\maroonD{50}$ games by dividing both parts of the ratio by $\purpleE{5}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{20\%}$ of $250$ is $\maroonD{50}$.

$\maroonD{150}\div\maroonD{50}=\purpleE{3}$

Now we can find the percentage for $\maroonD{150}$ games by multiplying both parts of the new ratio by $\purpleE{3}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{60\%}$ of $250$ is $\maroonD{150}$.

###Completed table

Games | Percentage

assessment_items.question_content:
The double number line shows that the distance from the start of a hiking trail to a cave entrance is $700\,\text{m}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of the distance.**

Distance ($\text{m}$) | Percentage
:-:|:-:
$700$ | [[☃ numeric-input 1]]$\%$ of $700\,\text{m}$
$175$ | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]$\%$ of $700\,\text{m}$
$350$ | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]$\%$ of $700\,\text{m}$
hints:
The number lines show that the ratio of $\maroonD{\text{meters}}$ to $\blueE{\text{percentage}}$ is $\maroonD{700}:\blueE{100\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\maroonD{700}:\blueE{100\%}$.

$\maroonD{700}\div \maroonD{175}= \purpleE{4}$

So, we can find the percentage for $\maroonD{175\,\text{m}}$ by dividing both parts of the ratio by $\purpleE{4}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{25\%}$ of $700$ is $\maroonD{175}$.

$\maroonD{350}\div\maroonD{175}=\purpleE{2}$

Now we can find the percentage for $\maroonD{350\,\text{m}}$ by multiplying both parts of the new ratio by $\purpleE{2}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So $\blueE{50\%}$ of $700$ is $\maroonD{350}$.

###Completed table

Distance ($\text{m}$) | Percentage
:-:|:-:
$700$ | $100\%$ of $700\,\text{m}$
$175$ | $25\%$ of $700\,\text{m}$
$350$ | $50\%$ of $700\,\text{m}$

assessment_items.question_content:
The tape diagram shows that Sanara gave $70$ haircuts this week.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of the haircuts.**

Haircuts | Percentage
-:|-:
$70$ | [[☃ numeric-input 1]]$\%$ of $70$ haircuts
$7$ | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]$\%$ of $70$ haircuts
$63$ | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]$\%$ of $70$ haircuts
hints:
The tape diagram shows that the ratio of haircuts to percentage is $\purpleD{70}:\purpleD{100\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\purpleD{70}:\purpleD{100\%}$.

###What percent is $\purpleD{70}$ out of $\purpleD{70}$ haircuts?

$\purpleD{70}$ haircuts is all of them, which is $\purpleD{100\%}$ of the haircuts.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueE{7}$ haircuts is $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{10}}$ of the haircuts.  [[☃ explanation 1]]

$\maroonE{63}$ haircuts is $\maroonE{\dfrac{9}{10}}$ of the haircuts.  [[☃ explanation 2]]

Now, we can use the same tape diagram to find $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{10}}$ of $\purpleD{100\%}$ and $\maroonE{\dfrac{9}{10}}$ of $\purpleD{100\%}$.

###What percent is $\blueE{7}$ out of $\purpleE{70}$ haircuts?

Each part in the tape diagram is $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{10}}$ of $\purpleD{100\%}$. So it's also $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{10}}$ of $\purpleD{70}$ haircuts.

$\purpleD{70}\times \blueE{\dfrac{1}{10}} = \blueE{7}$

Each part represents $\blueE{7}$ haircuts.

[[☃ image 2]]

So, the ratio $\purpleD{70}:\purpleD{100\%}$ is equivalent to $\blueE{7}:\blueE{10\%}$.

In other words, $\blueE{10\%}$ of $\purpleE{70}$ is $\blueE{7}$.

###What percent is $\maroonE{63}$ out of $\purpleE{70}$ haircuts?

$\maroonE{63}$ haircuts is $\maroonE{\dfrac{9}{10}}$ of the haircuts.

So it takes ${9}$ parts to represent $\maroonE{63}$ haircuts.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each part represents $\blueE{10\%}$.

$\blueE{10\%}\times {9} = \maroonE{90\%}$

So, the ratio $\purpleD{70}:\purpleD{100\%}$ is equivalent to $\maroonE{63}:\maroonE{90\%}$.

In other words, $\maroonE{90\%}$ of $\purpleE{70}$ is $\maroonE{63}$.

###Completed table

Haircuts | Percentage
-:|-:
$\purpleD{70}$ | $\purpleD{100\%}$ of $70$ haircuts
$\blueE{7}$ | $\blueE{10\%}$ of $70$ haircuts
$\maroonE{63}$ | $\maroonE{90\%}$ of $70$ haircuts

options.alt: A tape diagram with one tape split into 10 equal parts. All 10 parts are shaded. A curved brace above the entire tape is labeled 70. A curved brace below the entire tape is labeled 100 percent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The tape diagram shows that there are $155$ poems in a collection.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of the poems.**

Poems | Percentage
-:|-:
$155$ | [[☃ numeric-input 1]]$\%$ of $155$ poems
$31$ | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]$\%$ of $155$ poems
$186$ | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]$\%$ of $155$ poems
hints:
The tape diagram shows that the ratio of poems to percentage is $\purpleD{155}:\purpleD{100\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\purpleD{155}:\purpleD{100\%}$.

###What percent is $\purpleD{155}$ out of $\purpleD{155}$ poems?

$\purpleD{155}$ poems is all of them, which is $\purpleD{100\%}$ of the poems.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueE{31}$ poems is $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{5}}$ of the poems.  [[☃ explanation 1]]

$\maroonE{186}$ poems is $\maroonE{\dfrac{6}{5}}$ of the poems.  [[☃ explanation 2]]

Now, we can use the same tape diagram to find $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{5}}$ of $\purpleD{100\%}$ and $\maroonE{\dfrac{6}{5}}$ of $\purpleD{100\%}$.

###What percent is $\blueE{31}$ out of $\purpleE{155}$ poems?

Each part in the tape diagram is $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{5}}$ of $\purpleE{100\%}$. So it's also $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{5}}$ of $\purpleE{155}$ poems.

$\purpleE{155}\times \blueE{\dfrac{1}{5}} = \blueE{31}$

Each part represents $\blueE{31}$ poems.

[[☃ image 2]]

So, the ratio $\purpleE{155}:\purpleD{100\%}$ is equivalent to $\blueE{31}:\blueE{20\%}$.

In other words, $\blueE{20\%}$ of $\purpleE{155}$ is $\blueE{31}$.

###What percent is $\maroonE{186}$ out of $\purpleE{155}$ poems?

$\maroonE{186}$ poems is $\maroonE{\dfrac{6}{5}}$ of the poems.

So it takes ${6}$ parts to represent $\maroonE{186}$ poems.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each part represents $\blueE{20\%}$.

$\blueE{20\%}\times {6} = \maroonE{120\%}$

So, the ratio $\purpleE{155}:\purpleE{100\%}$ is equivalent to $\maroonE{186}:\maroonE{120\%}$.

In other words, $\maroonE{120\%}$ of $\purpleE{155}$ is $\maroonE{186}$.

This makes sense because $\maroonE{186}$ poems is more than $\purpleE{155}$ poems. So it should be more than $\purpleE{100\%}$ of the total poems.

###Completed table

Poems | Percentage
-:|-:
$\purpleE{155}$ | $\purpleE{100\%}$ of $\purpleE{155}$ poems
$\blueE{31}$ | $\blueE{20\%}$ of $\purpleE{155}$ poems
$\maroonE{186}$ | $\maroonE{120\%}$ of $\purpleE{155}$ poems

options.alt: A tape diagram with one tape split into 6 equal parts. The first 5 parts are shaded. A curved brace above the first 5 parts is labeled 155. A curved brace below the first 5 parts is labeled 100 percent.

assessment_items.question_content:
The tape diagram shows that there are $90$ teeth on a comb.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the table to show different percentages of the teeth on the comb.**

Teeth | Percentage

hints:
The tape diagram shows that the ratio of teeth to percentage is $\purpleD{90}:\purpleD{100\%}$.

We are looking for ratios that are equivalent to $\purpleD{90}:\purpleD{100\%}$.

###What percent is $\purpleD{90}$ out of $\purpleD{90}$ teeth?

$\purpleD{90}$ teeth is all of them, which is $\purpleD{100\%}$ of the teeth.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueE{9}$ teeth is $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{10}}$ of the teeth.  [[☃ explanation 1]]

$\maroonE{27}$ teeth is $\maroonE{\dfrac{3}{10}}$ of the teeth.  [[☃ explanation 2]]

Now, we can use the same tape diagram to find $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{10}}$ of $\purpleD{100\%}$ and $\maroonE{\dfrac{3}{10}}$ of $\purpleD{100\%}$.

###What percent is $\blueE{9}$ out of $\purpleE{90}$ teeth?

Each part in the tape diagram is $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{10}}$ of $\purpleD{100\%}$. So it's also $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{10}}$ of $\purpleD{90}$ teeth.

$\purpleD{90}\times \blueE{\dfrac{1}{10}} = \blueE{9}$

Each part represents $\blueE{9}$ teeth.

[[☃ image 2]]

So, the ratio $\purpleD{90}:\purpleD{100\%}$ is equivalent to $\blueE{9}:\blueE{10\%}$.

In other words, $\blueE{10\%}$ of $\purpleE{90}$ is $\blueE{9}$.

###What percent is $\maroonE{27}$ out of $\purpleE{90}$ teeth?

$\maroonE{27}$ teeth is $\maroonE{\dfrac{3}{10}}$ of the teeth.

So it takes ${3}$ parts to represent $\maroonE{27}$ teeth.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each part represents $\blueE{10\%}$.

$\blueE{10\%}\times {3} = \maroonE{30\%}$

So, the ratio $\purpleD{90}:\purpleD{100\%}$ is equivalent to $\maroonE{27}:\maroonE{30\%}$.

In other words, $\maroonE{30\%}$ of $\purpleE{90}$ is $\maroonE{27}$.

###Completed table

Teeth | Percentage
-:|-:
$\purpleD{90}$ | $\purpleD{100\%}$ of $\purpleD{90}$ teeth

$\maroonE{27}$ | $\maroonE{30\%}$ of $\purpleD{90}$ teeth
